repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1: shared-edge compatibility makes cellwise bounds nonsharp

published_prior_art.cite: SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 gives sharp bounds for direct/spillover effects in fixed canonical exposure settings, not latent sparse K-support shared-map frontiers
HuLiWager2022 links direct plus indirect effects to an infinitesimal Bernoulli policy effect under specified exposure structure, not joint nonrectangular partial-ID under latent exposure labels

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1: capacitated-incidence certificate characterization for fixed hidden-edge exposure endpoints

published_prior_art.cite: BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011, EdmondsMatchingPolytope1965, and Wolsey1975 supply random-set and polyhedral ingredients, not the sparse-network exposure endpoint certificate split

strengthening_paths.suggestion: In Stage 0, make the K=2,n=4 witness the first derivation target and explicitly compute both Gamma_2(u*;P*) and Delta_2(P*) before attempting the generic open-set persistence argument.

Add a short derivation note separating the inherited pid_network_exposure shared-completion obstruction from the genuinely new K-support label normalization and direct/indirect support-vector obstruction.

For Conjecture 3, specify the finite certificate language for arbitrary K-support exposure maps as functional-dependence constraints, not only hidden-edge incidence constraints, before reducing the K=2 binary case to b-matching/set cover.

recommended_next_step: Proceed to Stage 0, prioritizing the concrete witness computation as the gate for the flagship generic-class claims.
